political.s: Lumen's FEC donations show a right-leaning bias with $134K raised, skewing 83% Republican (29,500 of 35,500). The company maintains a bipartisan lobbying presence but weighted toward Republican interests. Recent data shows no outside spending in 2024.

charity.s: Lumen donated its Monroe campus to University of Louisiana Monroe in 2023, a major gift among largest received by public universities in Louisiana. Employee volunteer programs logged 18,000+ hours with $1.7M donated to nonprofits in 2022. Company matches employee gifts and sponsors education initiatives.

environment.s: Lumen has set science-based targets for emissions reduction aligned with 1.5°C climate goals and aims for net-zero by 2070. It procures 99% renewable electricity in EMEA regions and operates energy-efficient networks. DitchCarbon rates environmental sustainability at 44/100, indicating below-average performance despite commitments.

labor.s: Multiple NLRB cases filed against Lumen indicate labor disputes and potential unfair labor practice allegations. Company publishes diversity metrics showing 47% women and 50%+ people of color in 2021 hiring cohorts. Glassdoor ratings reflect mixed employee satisfaction with concerns about restructuring.

dei.s: Lumen has a Chief Diversity and Inclusion Officer and was named to Forbes' Best Employers for Diversity and Top Female Friendly Companies in 2022. Company partners with 200+ diverse organizations for recruitment and earned recognition for disability inclusion from DiversityComm and U.S. News.

animals.s: N/A - Lumen is a telecommunications company with no animal testing or product involvement.

guns.s: N/A - Lumen is a telecommunications and networking services provider with no firearms sales or manufacturing.

privacy.s: Lumen publishes semi-annual transparency reports disclosing government data demands and complies with GDPR, CCPA, and other privacy regulations. Company uses cookies and third-party analytics tracking but allows opt-out preferences. Practices are standard for telecom but involve extensive data collection.

execPay.s: CEO Kate Johnson earned $10.5M in 2024 compensation (87.8% stock/bonuses, 12.2% salary). Her pay exceeds market average for similar-sized companies and increased while company remained unprofitable. Pay-to-worker ratio not disclosed but CEO compensation significantly outpaces typical employee earnings.
